rulings.Case Title: WAUKEEN Q. MCCOY VS. KEN PAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 12, 2010, line 6. Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction. Preliminary injunction is granted. The hardship plaintiff will suffer, the loss of his home, is far greater than the harm defendants are likely to suffer if the injunction is denied. Plaintiff demonstrates a reasonable probability that he will prevail on the merits. Plaintiff shall post a bond in the amount of $100,000. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS ALJEJO et al VS. JACK O'CONNELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate And Oppos. To Resp.'S Mtn For Summary Judgment And Exhibits Vol 1-13; Exh'S 1-95 And Req. For Judicial Ntc; Appendix Of Federal Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 12, 2010, line 7. PETITIONERS LUIS ALJEJO, ANGELICA ARECHIGA, JOEL AVILA, OAXAQUENO BINACIONAL, CALIFORNIANS TOGETHER'S Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate is denied. The court finds that E.C. section 52177(d) remains operative. That code section does not mandate that any language other than English be taught and is not necessarily (quote) bilingual education.(close quote) A.B. 2587 specifically amended E.C. section 52177 while repealing numerous other statutes. A.B. 1107 (passed in 2001) amended E.C. section 52177 to remove subsection e. The legislature's actions show that E.C. section 52177(d) was to remain in effect. However, while E.C. section 52177 states that on site reviews shall occur at least every three years, E.C. 64001(b) states that (quote) the Superintendent shall establish the process and frequency for conducting reviews... (close quote) E.C. section 64001(b), as a specific provision, takes precedence over the conflicting general provision found in E.C. section 52177(d). People v. Vessell (1995) 36 Cal App 4th 285, 289. The evidence is insufficient to show an abuse of discretion by the Superintendent. Under E.C. section 64001, on site inspections must continue. Given the Superintendent's broad discretion, a short suspension does not qualify as an abuse of discretion, and it is uncontroverted that some onsite insepections have occurred from 2008-2010. (see Rico Deposition pg. 768; Rico delcaration; U.M.F. 10 and 13.) Petitioner does not show that Respondents are violating federal law. The E.E.O.C. requires California to take (quote) appropriate action to overcome language barriers that impede equal participation by its students in instructional programs.(close quote) 20 U.S.C. section 1703(f). The statute does not require on-site monitoring, and respondents are still taking steps to protect L.E.P. students while trying to fine-tune the monitoring program. Petitioner has not demonstrated an abandonment that violates
